title: The Three-Sentence Masterpiece
content: Hardest email I ever wrote? A client who was furious. Totally valid reasons. I spent two hours on three sentences. They replied: 'This is the most professional response I've ever received.' Sometimes less is more.
extended_content: They had every right to be angry. We'd dropped the ball badly. My first draft was five paragraphs of explanation and apology. Deleted it. Second draft was two paragraphs. Still too much. The final version: acknowledged the problem, took responsibility, stated exactly what we'd do to fix it. Three sentences. No excuses. No deflection. Just ownership and action. That client stayed with us for another five years.
when_to_tell: when helping with difficult or emotional emails
lesson: In difficult situations, brevity shows confidence and respect
